title: Recruiter Evidence Eval Example
description: One human-reviewable dataset row for the V1 profile-only candidate-evidence chatbot. Keep rows small: one recruiter-style question, one expected behavior, and short notes for labeling.

schemaVersion.description: Schema contract version for dataset rows.

exampleId.description: Stable row identifier. Use readable slugs such as prod-ai-001 or k8s-evidence-001.

question.description: The exact user-facing recruiter prompt to send to the chatbot.

requestClass.description: Coarse category for what kind of request the question is making.

oneOf.description: The question should be answerable from profile.md.
The question asks for a claim that profile.md does not support, or supports only with a caveat.
The prompt is outside recruiter-evidence Q&A, including inert prompt-injection canaries or tool/write requests.

expectedBehavior.description: What a passing answer should do at a coarse behavior level.

oneOf.description: Answer, but make the profile-supported limitation explicit.
Say profile.md does not support the requested claim.
Refuse or redirect because the prompt is off-contract, abusive, unsafe, or asks for tool/write behavior.

sourceSupport.description: How strongly profile.md should support the requested claim. This guides labeling; it is not a second rubric.

oneOf.description: profile.md should support a direct answer.
profile.md supports adjacent or narrower evidence, so the answer needs a caveat.
profile.md does not support the requested claim.
Source support is not the main issue, usually for off-topic or abuse rows.

expectedAnswerNotes.description: Short reviewer-facing notes about what a passing answer should do. This is not a golden/reference answer.

mustAvoid.description: Optional short list of claims, phrases, or implications that should make the response fail if they appear or are implied.

productionAiProbe.description: True when the row probes production AI experience, ownership, shipping, operations, customer scale, or adjacent production-readiness claims.
